current_open_surface_state: Direct FTC PDFs for complaint/order/motion surfaces are already captured and usable with posture caveats.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: The host-local FTC legal-library shell remains anti-automation blocked, so shell visibility and PDF visibility are different states.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can safely say the FTC rows have direct official PDF text even though the case-library shell is blocked from this host.
what_real_progress_would_be: A live public case page, docket summary, or another reviewer-checkable official shell that aligns with the already-captured PDFs without weakening chronology caution.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not flatten blocked shell access into missing official documents, and do not flatten direct-PDF reachability into full case-page discoverability or stable chronology closure.
visualization_hook: Blocked-source panel; source-family saturation methods note; official-shell-vs-direct-document badge
paper_thesis_use: Methods paragraph on why blocked shells do not always mean blocked official evidence.

current_open_surface_state: EEOC summary pages expose a stable civil-action number and settlement/decree summary from the agency side.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: No captured public court-originating complaint, decree, docket entry, or judicial order text is present in the current local package.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can say the EEOC publicly announced suit and later settlement/decree terms, but the row remains agency-summary-level rather than court-text-supported.
what_real_progress_would_be: One genuinely different public court-facing complaint, consent decree, docket entry, or judicial order text from an official or clearly labeled open mirror.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not treat agency summaries as court-text substitutes, and do not repeat exhausted EEOC/newsroom/search paths as if they were a new depth gain.
visualization_hook: Route-B blocker panel; procedural-posture timeline; thin-family badge
paper_thesis_use: Table 1A limitations note and hostile-reviewer defense for court-depth absence.

current_open_surface_state: A DOJ-hosted indictment PDF is publicly captured and hashed, so charging-document presence is real.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: The current host treats the PDF as image-only for practical extraction, and exact caption/case-number normalization remains partial.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can say the Nate matter has an official DOJ-linked charging document plus accusation-stage summary support, but not that full caption metadata is normalized.
what_real_progress_would_be: A materially different OCR stack, better public original, or another public exact-caption source that recovers caption metadata without inventing unread digits.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not flatten PDF presence into normalized court-text depth, and do not promote unread suffix digits into a case number.
visualization_hook: Normalization-stage overlay; blocked-source panel; court-depth methods note
paper_thesis_use: Methods/thesis note distinguishing discovery success from extraction failure.

current_open_surface_state: Amsterdam exposes rich register fields and Helsinki has a same-buyer procurement package with IBM-consistent award metadata.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: Amsterdam still lacks a reviewer-checkable exact buyer-side tender/award/contract or public assessment tied to the named row; Helsinki remains family-level rather than page-specific procurement proof.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can support a disclosure-surface heterogeneity claim and a traceability ladder, but not page-specific procurement, spend, supplier, or impact-assessment proof for the register rows.
what_real_progress_would_be: One static official procurement, contract, tender-lot, DPIA, impact-assessment, or public companion page tied exactly to Amsterdam or page-specifically to the Helsinki parking-chatbot row.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not flatten governance fields into procurement proof, and do not flatten same-buyer continuity into exact row-linked procurement evidence.
visualization_hook: Table 1B traceability ladder; jurisdiction/language map; Route-B blocker panel
paper_thesis_use: Public-sector observability chapter and reviewer-safe proof-ceiling wording.

current_open_surface_state: The live security package already has vendor, CVE, CERT, independent-lab, and comparator structure, plus one singleton trust-boundary row.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: No incident-specific peer-reviewed, workshop, or conference-grade public technical case attachment is yet mapped to a live row.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can safely present evidence-layer asymmetry and mechanism/remediation heterogeneity, but not balanced scholarly authority coverage.
what_real_progress_would_be: One incident-specific peer-reviewed, workshop, or conference-grade public technical attachment to a live row; only after that should breadth or notice-depth polishing retake priority.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not count generic agent-security literature, framework overlap, or more Langflow/CrewAI notice polishing as closure of the scholarly gap.
visualization_hook: Table 6 authority-balance panel; evidence-layer Sankey; Route-B blocker panel
paper_thesis_use: Security methods section and reviewer-safe explanation of why source roles do not yet balance.

current_open_surface_state: Two Springer-linked direct notice-text exemplars exist, and Crossref/PubMed corroboration improves blocked-notice metadata for NEJM/SAGE rows.
blocked_or_limited_surface_state: SAGE remains mediated and the NEJM direct publisher notice text remains blocked from this lane, so top-depth notice diversity is still publisher-concentrated.
strongest_safe_claim_now: LIMEN can support a bounded source-depth and blocked-notice asymmetry claim, but not a broad cross-publisher direct-notice depth claim.
what_real_progress_would_be: One direct publisher-originating notice text from a non-Springer row, or another materially different public publisher surface that improves text-level diversity without overstating editorial reasoning.
what_not_to_flatten: Do not flatten Crossref/PubMed role-status corroboration into direct publisher notice readability, and do not flatten Springer depth into publisher-diverse closure.
visualization_hook: Table 1E source-depth panel; publisher-notice upgrade funnel; methods limitations note
paper_thesis_use: Procedural-contamination / research-integrity section on source-depth asymmetry across publishers.
